entities.name: AFRICAN EGGPLANT
entities.description: A nutritious traditional vegetable (Solanum aethiopicum) used in many African countries.

entities.name: UGANDA
entities.description: An African country where the African eggplant is used.

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: An African country where the African eggplant is used.

entities.name: SOLANUM ANGUIVI
entities.description: The wild relative from which the African eggplant is thought to have been domesticated.

entities.name: DISEASE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist diseases, for which S. aethiopicum is a source of genes.

entities.name: SOLANACEAE
entities.description: The plant family to which the African eggplant and Solanum melongena belong.

entities.name: SOLANUM MELONGENA
entities.description: A crop within the Solanaceae family that uses S. aethiopicum as a source of disease resistance genes.

entities.name: GENOMIC RESOURCES
entities.description: The set of genomic data and tools used to facilitate breeding.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The process of improving the genetic traits of a crop.

entities.name: DRAFT GENOME
entities.description: The 1.02-Gb assembled genome sequence of Solanum aethiopicum.

entities.name: REPETITIVE SEQUENCES
entities.description: Sequences that make up 78.9% of the S. aethiopicum draft genome.

entities.name: GENE MODELS
entities.description: The 37,681 annotated gene models identified in the draft genome.

entities.name: PROTEIN-CODING GENES
entities.description: The 34,906 genes in the draft genome that code for proteins.

entities.name: LONG TERMINAL REPEAT RETROTRANSPOSONS
entities.description: Genetic elements whose amplification led to the expansion of disease resistance genes.

entities.name: RESEQUENCING
entities.description: The process of sequencing multiple genotypes to identify genetic variations.

entities.name: SINGLE-NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISMS
entities.description: Genetic variations identified through resequencing, used by breeders for selection.

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild plants for human use.

entities.name: DEMOGRAPHIC HISTORY
entities.description: The study of population changes over time in a species.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand drought conditions.

entities.name: GILO
entities.description: A group of S. aethiopicum showing active selection for drought tolerance genes.

entities.name: SHUM
entities.description: A group of S. aethiopicum showing active selection for drought tolerance genes.

entities.name: PAN-GENOME
entities.description: An assembled genome containing the entire set of genes from multiple S. aethiopicum genotypes.

entities.name: REFERENCE GENOME
entities.description: The baseline genome sequence used for comparison with other genotypes.

entities.name: BIOTIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: Resistance to living organisms such as pests and diseases.

entities.name: ABIOTIC RESISTANCE
entities.description: Resistance to non-living environmental stressors such as drought.
